Smart strategy with regime detection, multi-timeframe analysis,
grid trading, trend following, and risk management.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add Supabase + IndexedDB for local-first cloud sync

Architecture: localStorage → IndexedDB → Supabase (background)
- Offline: full functionality via IndexedDB
- Online: auto-sync dirty data to Supabase
- Cache clear recovery: restore from Supabase

New files:
- lib/supabase.ts: Supabase client
- lib/db.ts: IndexedDB (Dexie.js) CRUD + localStorage migration
- lib/sync.ts: sync layer (dirty tracking, cloud push/pull)
- components/SyncProvider.tsx: init migration + auto-sync
- supabase/setup.sql: table definitions

Changes:
- store.ts: every save() also writes to IndexedDB + triggers sync
- layout.tsx: wraps app in SyncProvider

commit.message:
win32: strip dead POSIX includes + add M_PI guard for MSVC

All model files (parakeet, canary, canary_ctc, cohere, granite,
qwen3, voxtral, voxtral4b) had `#include <sys/mman.h>`, `<unistd.h>`,
`<fcntl.h>`, `<sys/stat.h>` left over from before the core_gguf
migration. These were dead — no mmap/munmap/open/close/MAP_SHARED
call sites remained. Removing them unblocks MSVC compilation
(error C1083: Cannot open include file: 'sys/mman.h').

Also adds `#ifndef M_PI / #define M_PI ... / #endif` to all files
that use M_PI in FFT code. MSVC doesn't define M_PI unless
`_USE_MATH_DEFINES` is set before `<cmath>`, and guarding it
ourselves is more portable than forcing the define.

README: regenerate feature matrix to show cross-cutting capabilities
(LID pre-step available for ALL backends via `-l auto`, diarize
available for ALL backends via `--diarize`, word-level timing via
`-am` aligner for LLM backends).

commit.message:
feat: add narrate serve — OpenAI-compatible TTS server (#9)

* feat: add `narrate serve` — OpenAI-compatible TTS HTTP server

Adds POST /v1/audio/speech endpoint that accepts { model?, voice?, input }
and returns OGG Opus audio bytes. Port 3100 by default (configurable via
PORT env or --port flag). This enables vault's webhook trigger system to
fire TTS requests at narrate as a standalone service.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: return server object from startServer, test against real server

Self-review caught that tests were duplicating handler logic instead of
exercising the actual server. Now startServer() returns the Bun.serve
result so tests (and callers) can get the port and stop it gracefully.
Tests use startServer(0) to bind a random port and hit the real handler.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: bind to localhost only, update CLAUDE.md for serve command

Review nits: localhost-only service shouldn't bind 0.0.0.0, and CLAUDE.md
still said narrate intentionally has no HTTP server.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
docs: add §9 rollout sequence for Phase 3.5

Captures the PR-level workflow for landing the hook-based response
boundary detection:

- PR 1 (this PR): design doc. Regex detector + placeholder stay as
  fallback for non-Claude runners.
- PR 2: spike findings in docs/plans/notes/hook-spike.md, +/- any
  revisions to §4 the empirical findings require. Go/no-go gate.
- PR 3: Phase 3.5.B-F implementation in one PR (sub-phases are
  sequential + not independently testable, so they're commits
  inside one PR rather than separate PRs). Real-Claude end-to-end
  test replaces or joins the cat-based smoke test.

Also documents the contingency if the spike returns red (pivot to
Tier 2 with a new design doc), and calls out what work is parallel
to this rollout (Phases 4-6, Council Phase 1+2, not Council Phase 3
which is gated on ResponseComplete firing).

commit.message:
Add Quick Mosh panel, global mode, cascade, debug logger, and I-frame kill fixes

Core encoder fixes:
- Kill B-frames (bframes=0, b-adapt=0) when killIFrames is active so x264
  lookahead can no longer insert I-frames, making the datamosh smear persist
- Fix min-keyint to match keyint (was min-keyint=1, letting x264 insert
  keyframes freely despite large keyint setting)
- Fix MBEditOnly path overwriting killIFrames pict_type hint with NONE

Cascade system:
- Replace exponential per-frame decay formula with linear fade over cascadeLen:
  f = 1 - (cascadeDecay/100) * (k/cascadeLen). Old formula killed the cascade
  in ~12-21 frames regardless of cascadeLen (e.g. decay=35 died at frame 21
  of a 333-frame cascade). New formula respects the full cascade length.
- Fix MV Melt preset refDepth=0->1 so mvDrift actually has a reference frame

Quick Mosh panel (QuickMoshWidget):
- New panel with 10 named datamosh presets and "Mosh Now" button
- Cascade presets: Ghost Smear, MV Liquify →/↓, Chroma Bleed, Vortex,
  Scatter Dissolve, Full Freeze
- Per-frame stamp presets: Pixel Disintegrate, UV Colour Twist, Block Melt
- Auto-enforces killIFrames=true and gopSize=0 (infinite GOP) on every apply

MacroblockWidget enhancements:
- Global mode: empty selectedMBs now applies edits to all MBs in the frame
- Active frame range: knob changes propagate to all timeline-selected frames
- Cascade sliders exempt from range propagation (prevents cascade self-blocking)
- 8 per-frame presets with correct refDepth values
- loadEditMap() for Quick Mosh to push edit state into the widget

PreviewPlayer:
- WMF cache-bust (clear source before setting new) for reliable reload
- Auto-play after Apply so results are immediately visible

ControlLogger:
- New debug logging module (core/logger/) for knob/apply pipeline tracing
- Writes to build/Debug/logs/LaMoshPit_ControlTest_Log.txt when enabled

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Complete publication report v2 test suite — 14 tests covering

* fix: remove unused imports in test_publication_export_v2

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: resolve figure quality issues from manual review

- OPLS-DA score plot: update title from "Scores Plot" to "OPLS-DA Scores Plot"
- VIP plot: add missing suptitle "PLS-DA VIP Scores"
- Heatmap: move seaborn colorbar from top-left (occluding dendrogram) to
  bottom-right via cbar_pos=(0.88, 0.06, 0.03, 0.16)
- AUC ranking: replace legend() with ax.text() annotations on each vline,
  eliminating overlay on the bars

- ANOVA boxplot: move stat annotation from fig.text(x=0.02) to

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: correct colorbar and AUC label placement after manual review

- Heatmap: switch to post-draw repositioning via cluster_grid.cax.set_position()
  anchored to ax_heatmap.get_position(), ensuring colorbar never overlaps
  the rotated x-axis (MZ/RT) labels regardless of label count
- AUC ranking: move "Random"/"Good" annotations to ax.transAxes coordinates
  (y=0.05, va=bottom) placed after invert_yaxis(), so position is independent

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: move AUC ranking reference lines to upper-right legend

Replace ax.text() annotations on vlines with a proper legend using

placement in the empty corner above the top-ranked bar.

loc="lower right", bbox_to_anchor=(1.0, 1.0) so the legend's bottom

between the title and the first bar.

reach top) to y=1.02/va=bottom/clip_on=False (outside axes, floating
in the gap between plot and title). Add rect=[0,0,1,0.90] to
tight_layout to guarantee space for the floating annotation.

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

aprendizado de docs via link, fluxo contínuo e observação contextual

aprendizado_comandos.py:
- Nova tabela docs_bots: armazena documentação bruta + resumo gerado pela IA
- importar_documentacao(): busca URL, chama Groq para extrair comandos
  estruturados (prefixo, slash, lista de cmds), persiste em comandos_bots
  e bots_conhecidos com os dados reais do doc
- consultar_documentacao(): retorna resumo da doc salva por bot
- contexto_completo_para_ia(): agora inclui bloco [DOCUMENTAÇÃO IMPORTADA
  DE BOTS] no context de aprendizado injetado no system prompt

responder_auto_silencioso.py:
- _buscar_conteudo_completo_url(): fetch HTML completo com remoção de tags
  (scripts, nav, footer), retorna texto limpo para análise (max 18k chars)
- _RE_INTENT_DOC: regex que detecta "doc", "manual", "aprende", "leia",
  "guia", "comandos" + URL → intent de ensinar bot
- _processar_doc_bot(): orquestra fetch + import + confirmação no canal
- Detecção no canal: proprietário + link + intent → dispara importação
- Detecção na DM: proprietário + link → dispara importação sem precisar
  mencionar o bot explicitamente
- _fluxo_bot_ativo: estado por canal para encadear etapas com bots
- Prompt de interação com bot: instrução de FLUXO CONTÍNUO (encadeia
  etapas sem esperar nova ordem) e DOCUMENTAÇÃO IMPORTADA (usa sintaxe
  exata dos comandos aprendidos)

commit.message:
refactor(backend_openai): unify caps_preview + caps into single lookup (#846)

backend_openai.build_request had TWO Capabilities.for_model_id
lookups that both fell back to default_capabilities:

  let caps_preview = ... (* for max_tokens clamp *)
  ...
  let caps = ...          (* for top_k/min_p gates *)

Both computed the exact same value. Unify into a single [caps]
binding at the top of the sampling block that drives both the
max_tokens clamp and the sampling gates.

Also adds a WARN when the clamp fires (max_tokens > cap), using
the existing warn_capability_drop helper with a "max_tokens:clamp"
field sentinel so the drop-vs-clamp distinction is visible in logs.

Supersedes the conflicted portions of #844.

No semantic change for any existing test — all tests green. The
max_tokens clamp logic is unchanged (it was already on main); only
the redundant lookup is removed and the clamp path now WARNs.

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Time display rules: 現地時間/日本時間, no raw timezone abbrevs

- formatTimeDisplay() helper for consistent time formatting
- isInternational() to detect non-domestic timezones

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Remove og:url to fix X cache busting

X caches card scrapes against the og:url canonical value, not the
URL in the tweet. Our og:url was always the bare /starr-ai path,
so ?v=5 through ?v=8 all resolved to the same cached entry and
never triggered a fresh scrape.

Removing og:url forces X to cache against the actual tweet URL,
making ?v=N query params work as real cache busters.

Mid-cycle restart now starts all loops immediately (flip, stop, take
profit all active) but blocks NEW entries until next cycle boundary.
Previous version blocked everything for 10+ min — dangerous with
6 contracts held and no protection against regime change.

commit.message:
crypto_15m: fix double-entry + disable XRP

1. Remove overzealous "absorbed by residuals" state reset in fill
   handler. It was resetting direction + entry_made on normal fills,
   causing the fast loop to re-detect direction and enter AGAIN.
   Positions don't carry across cycles — no residuals to absorb.

2. Disable XRP — direction_min_dist of $0.00037 triggers on noise,
   enters at 60c, market prices at 25c, instant 35c hard stop.
   Need live calibration before enabling.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: wgpu sqrt bit-exactness via normalized Markstein algorithm

naga (wgpu's shader translator) doesn't support SPV_KHR_float_controls
and emits imprecise sqrt(), causing 1-ULP differences at fp16 precision
in radial gradients.  Rather than emit GLSLstd450Sqrt for the wgpu
path, we now emit a correctly-rounded software sqrt:

  1. Normalize x to [1,4) by extracting and replacing the exponent
     via integer bit manipulation.
  2. Compute rsqrt(xn) on the normalized value, where the hardware
     rsqrt estimate has full precision.
  3. Apply the Markstein correction using FMA:
       s  = xn * yn
       r  = fma(-s, s, xn)
       s  = fma(r, yn * 0.5, s)
  4. Scale back by adding half the original exponent to the result's
     exponent bits.
  5. Guard x == 0 with select.

Verified bit-exact against Metal's precise::sqrt for all 2 billion
positive normal floats.  Golden tests now pass with tol=0 across all
5 backends.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
crypto_15m: startup guard — wait for cycle boundary if mid-cycle

If agent starts between min 1-14 of a cycle, waits for the next
cycle boundary before trading. Prevents double-entry on restarts
within the same cycle (previous process may have already bought).

Safe windows: min 14-15 (cycle ending, no new buys) or min 0-1
(fresh cycle, clean market).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(backend_ollama): add capability gate for top_k / min_p (#840)

Follow-up to the #830/#831 serializer-gate series. backend_ollama.ml
was the last live request builder that still emitted [top_k] / [min_p]
unconditionally from [config.*]. Adding the gate here closes the
capability-consumer contract so every OAS serializer now respects
[Capabilities.for_model_id].

Behavioural change is intentionally zero for the common path:

  * [ollama_capabilities] inherits [supports_top_k = true] and
    [supports_min_p = true] from [openai_chat_extended_capabilities],
    so the default Ollama + qwen3 combination is byte-identical
    before and after this change.

  * The gate only fires when an operator explicitly lowers the
    capability record for a specific Ollama variant. In that case
    the one-shot WARN helper from Backend_openai (from #830) fires
    once per (model_id, field) — the same dedup table is shared
    across backend_openai, api_openai, and now backend_ollama.

Relation to #839:
The author of #839 reported Ollama-via-OpenAI-compat rejecting min_p
and proposed flipping [ollama_capabilities.supports_min_p = false].
That flip targets the wrong capability record (native Ollama goes
through backend_ollama, not through [ollama_capabilities] via
backend_openai — see #839 review for full analysis). This PR does
not change any capability value; it only adds the defensive gate so
any future capability flip (correct or otherwise) actually takes
effect on the native path.

Full dune runtest test green. Comment block explains the rationale.

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
exige autorização da equipe antes de qualquer punição

- System prompt: nova seção "PUNIÇÕES — REGRA DE OURO" — bot não pode
  silenciar/banir/expulsar por decisão própria; infração detectada deve
  ser reportada à equipe para ela decidir; exceções: racismo explícito
  e flood técnico automatizado (zero-tolerância pré-autorizada)
- Regra absoluta #12 adicionada: punições exigem autorização explícita
  da equipe competente; bot é suporte operacional, não juiz autônomo
- Violações (count >= 3): em vez de auto-silenciar, envia alerta ao
  canal de auditoria com menção ao cargo de moderação (CARGO_EQUIPE_MOD_ID)
  aguardando decisão; avisa o membro que o caso foi encaminhado
- Flood repetido (>= 3x): idem — notifica equipe no canal de auditoria

commit.message:
Initial commit: HandoffKit plugin for Claude Code

Minimal setup wizard plugin that connects Claude Code projects to
HandoffKit via MCP. One skill (/handoffkit:setup) that exchanges a
setup token for an API key and runs claude mcp add --scope local.

The plugin is disposable scaffolding — once setup is done, the MCP
connection persists independently. The plugin just provides the
/handoffkit:setup skill for linking new projects.

commit.message:
crypto_15m: fix residual position state corruption

1. seed_open_positions no longer sets ticker_contracts from old
   positions — only logs them. Prevents stale counts from causing
   oversells (SOL sold 5x when we only bought 2).

2. Fill handler resets BRTI state (held_side, entry_made, direction)
   when post_position_fp shows 0 after a buy — means our buy was
   absorbed closing residual positions, not opening new ones.

commit.message:
Implement OCR UI rules: category fields, inferred/review flags

Phase 1: Data model
- Step type: add inferred[], needsReview flags
- ocr-rules.ts: category-specific fixed field definitions
  (9 categories with appropriate fields per type)
- checkNeedsReview() for programmatic review detection
- API prompt returns inferred/needsReview per step

Phase 2: /scan + StepEditModal
- Category-specific fixed fields (not one-size-fits-all)
- Inferred fields show orange '推定' badge
- Empty fields show dashed red border (not blank)
- needsReview steps show yellow warning banner
- Both scan and modal use getFixedFields() from ocr-rules

Phase 3: /trips/[id] timeline
- needsReview steps show '要確認' mark + yellow dot
- endDate displayed (e.g., hotel IN→OUT dates)

commit.message:
refactor: collapse to single n2-standard-64 mongo host

Replace the two n2-standard-32 pair with one n2-standard-64: same
total capacity (64 vCPU / 256 GiB), cost-neutral at the workshop-day
level, and far simpler to operate.  The motivating reason is that
ltvm's microVM bridge (fcbr0 / 192.168.100.0/24) is host-local, so
splitting the workshop across two hosts would break any exercise
that needs a multi-node Lustre cluster spanning both machines.
Failover no longer argues for it either — a second host only helps
if the first dies, which we handle via a DR snapshot + cold-spare
reserved IP instead.

bin/create-account:
- Drops HOSTS=(host1 host2), the provision_one() dispatcher, the
  for-loop, and the REMOTE_SCRIPT heredoc.  The provisioning steps
  now run inline in the main script's shell -- no cross-host ssh,
  no $-escaping gymnastics, no "remote vs local" dispatch.  Net
  -48 lines, same behaviour on the one-host path.
- Refuses on duplicate username (default) or --force-password
  (recovery) are unchanged; pubkey paste with private-key-paste
  detection is unchanged; pubkey REPLACES on force-password is
  unchanged.

Docs:
- WORKSHOP.md: full rewrite for one mongo host.  VM table is one
  row + cold-spare IP.  DNS is two names (devday1 live, devday2
  cold spare).  Cost section redone for n2-standard-64 (~$25/day
  workshop, ~$57/mo idle with one 512 GB disk + 2 reserved IPs).
  Snapshot reframed as DR rather than multi-host deploy.  Calls
  out the cross-host-microvm-bridge limitation as the reason this
  isn't just a cost decision.
- README.md: architecture-in-one-paragraph rewritten for one host.
- ATTENDEE_FLOW.md: drops "both hosts" language, simplifies the
  create-account output example, rewrites the failover
  troubleshooting block honestly ("ask an organizer, there's no
  self-service failover for a dead host").

GCP state (already applied before this commit):
- Released devday-host3 and devday-host4 reserved IPs.  Only
  devday-host1 (live, 34.61.47.200) and devday-host2 (cold spare,
  35.232.23.141) remain reserved.

bd state (already updated, will be synced via `bd dolt push`):
- devday-saq (instantiate host2) closed as not needed
- devday-6dl (hand-configure) retitled + description rewritten
- devday-bgd (snapshot) reframed as disaster recovery
- devday-9ou (DNS) retitled for two names
- devday-59t (smoke test) dep rewired from closed devday-saq to
  devday-6dl; description updated for single-host checklist

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
eleva inteligência: autocrítica, janela deslizante, 70b para proprietário

- _resposta_parece_generica(): heurística local que detecta respostas
  off-topic/genéricas (saudação isolada, resposta curtíssima a pergunta
  longa, zero overlap de keywords) sem custo de API adicional
- Loop de autocrítica em responder_com_groq: quando a heurística dispara,
  regenera automaticamente com 70b → scout → qwen (modelo superior ao
  que gerou a resposta ruim), temperature=0.55 para resposta mais focada
- Janela deslizante com compressão: ao passar de 14 msgs no histórico,
  as 8 mais antigas são resumidas em 1-3 frases via 8b e injetadas como
  [RESUMO DA CONVERSA ANTERIOR] — o fio não se perde quando a janela gira
- _comprimir_historico(): função async que produz o resumo via Groq 8b
- Proprietário → 70b forçado: quando nivel=="PROPRIETÁRIO" e 70b tem
  budget (< 85% do TPD diário), ignora a cascata padrão e usa o melhor
  modelo disponível; fallback para scout se 70b esgotado

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add release workflow: build binaries on tag push

Triggers on v* tags. Builds release binaries for:
- macOS aarch64 (Apple Silicon, --all-features including metrics)
- Linux x86_64 (--features tui only, no macmon)

Packages each as a .tar.gz and creates a GitHub Release with
auto-generated release notes and the binaries attached.

commit.message:
Persist Vinted display name + add Switch RP account button

- Schema: rp_sessions.vinted_name column (additive migration)
- Extension: reads Vinted login from /api/v2/users/current during
  sync and POSTs it in /api/session/store, so the bot can show the
  display name without ever probing Vinted from Railway
- doLogin: reads session.vintedName from DB, no Vinted probe, and
  now replaces c.accounts rather than pushing — matches the
  one-chat-one-RP-user invariant
- Login/logout: clears rp_users.telegram_chat_id on unbind so the
  ensureLoaded fallback doesn't silently re-link the previous user
- Main menu: new "Switch RelistPro account" button + handler
- fetchVintedAccounts / hydrateVintedNamesFromExtension: read
  vinted_name from the DB, no more Railway→Vinted probe loops

fix: track relic hook runtime and auto-refresh model

Add the bundled hook files to the repo and refresh derived model artifacts automatically after successful capture.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Use household-based data model to match iOS app

The iOS app stores Plaid access tokens linked to households, not
individual users (tokens have user_id="unknown"). Users are grouped
into households where all members share the same connected banks.

- Add lib/household-helpers.ts with getPlaidTokensForUser() that
  resolves the user's household and returns all shared tokens
- Update /api/plaid/accounts, /api/plaid/transactions, /api/plaid/
  investments to use the household helper instead of direct user_id
  lookup

Verified: resolves 24 tokens for Brown Family household correctly.

commit.message:
Update default impact levels for three Level AA criteria

1.3.4 Orientation: Baseline → Moderate (blocker for fixed-mount device users)
2.4.11 Focus Appearance: Baseline → Moderate (quality of focus indicator matters)
3.3.4 Error Prevention: Baseline → Moderate (significant for legal/financial actions)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

crypto_15m: simplified strategy + 4 coins + speed + lock + spike detection

MAJOR REWRITE — simplified high-conviction strategy:

Strategy:
- Enter on sBRTI direction ($20+ from strike)
- Mathematical lock detection: knows when settlement is guaranteed
- Conviction adds when locked (aggressive, any positive distance)
- Take profit at 95c, re-entry at ≤59c
- Hold to settlement — NO trailing stop, NO protect profit
- One flip per cycle (regime change $100+ BTC wrong side)
- Hard stop at 35c loss (emergency only)
- NO churn, NO noise selling

Speed:
- Fast loop (500ms) uses ONLY cached WS prices — zero blocking API calls
- Flip orders use synchronous fast-path (not asyncio.to_thread) for
  minimum latency on regime changes when orderbook is stale
- Other orders use asyncio.to_thread to keep event loop responsive

Spike handling:
- detect_spike(): monitors 5s price range for extreme moves
- Lock invalidation: if spot moves >10x expected max in 5s, lock is
  invalidated (assumptions don't hold in extreme regime)
- Flip limit reset: spike allows additional flips beyond the 1-per-cycle
  limit for double-reversal scenarios ($200 drop then $300 spike)

Coins: BTC + ETH + SOL + XRP with proportionally scaled thresholds.
All dollar thresholds scaled by price ratio to BTC.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
